title: Progress Around the Boone-Higman Conjecture
speaker: Matthew Zaremsky
abstract: The Boone-Higman conjecture (1973) predicts that a finitely generated group has solvable word problem if and only if it embeds in a finitely presented simple group. The "if" direction is true and easy, but the "only if" direction has been open for over 50 years. In this talk I will discuss an interesting sufficient condition for a group to satisfy the conjecture, which does not require one to care about simple groups, but rather to care about group actions with certain properties. This viewpoint has led to a number of recent breakthroughs on the conjecture, such as proving it for all hyperbolic groups, and for the groups Aut(F_n). In all these cases the simple groups that arise are certain groups of Cantor space homeomorphisms with interesting dynamics, called "twisted Brin-Thompson groups". This talk will touch on joint work with combinations of Jim Belk, Collin Bleak, Francesco Fournier-Facio, James Hyde, and Francesco Matucci.
tags: Joint IAS/PU Groups and Dynamics Seminar

proof_stages.label: Boone-Higman formulation and easy converse
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates the Boone-Higman conjecture, unpacks simplicity and solvable word problem, explains why embedding in a finitely presented simple group implies solvable word problem, and recalls the Boone-Higman-Thompson two-step embedding theorem.

proof_stages.label: Known progress and the Aut(F_n) target
proof_stages.summary: The speaker surveys known classes satisfying the conjecture, states the new result for Aut(F_n), notes consequences for groups virtually embedding in Aut(F_n), and lists related open cases.

proof_stages.label: Type-A action criterion and twisted Brin-Thompson targets
proof_stages.summary: A sufficient condition is introduced: if a finitely presented group acts faithfully on a set with finitely generated point stabilizers and finitely many orbits on pairs, then it embeds in a finitely presented simple twisted Brin-Thompson group; this motivates the permutational Boone-Higman conjecture.

proof_stages.label: Aut(F_n) embedded into a larger automorphism group action
proof_stages.summary: Rather than use the natural Aut(F_n) action, the proof embeds Aut(F_n) in Gamma = Aut_G(G * F_n) and lets Gamma act by precomposition on S = Hom_G(G * F_n,G), a set naturally identified with G^n.

proof_stages.label: Mixed identity freeness and the main Aut(F_n) theorem
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines mixed identity free groups and states that if G is finitely presented, simple, and MIF, then the above Gamma-action is of type A, so Gamma and hence Aut(F_n) embed in a finitely presented simple twisted Brin-Thompson group.

proof_stages.label: Verification of the four type-A conditions
proof_stages.summary: Finite presentability is obtained from existing results, finite generation of stabilizers by computation, faithfulness from the MIF condition, and finite pair-orbit behavior from simplicity via products of conjugates that clear entries.

proof_stages.label: Permutational versus classical Boone-Higman
proof_stages.summary: The final argument shows that finitely presented simple MIF target groups themselves embed in the constructed type-A setting, relates this to twisted Brin-Thompson groups and highly transitive simple groups, and frames the open question of whether the classical and permutational conjectures coincide.
